---
title: "Hierarchical Subclass Metadata & HTR Scores"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Hierarchical Subclass Metadata & HTR Scores}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

## Why this vignette exists

Two related things landed on 2026-06-02:

1. A single-source-of-truth helper, `normalize_hier_assignments()`, that
   enforces the project-wide convention for `hier_*_assignment` labels
   (spaces → underscores; empty strings → `NA`).
2. A short reminder of what `data-raw/build_htr_scores.R` produces and how
   the new helper threads through it.

This vignette captures both so future-Scott (and future-Chloe) don't have
to reverse-engineer the wiring.

---

## 1. The hier_* convention

`headBCI::sheets$cleanMD` carries **28 `hier_*` columns** populated by the
headBCI sheets pipeline. The four *assignment* columns are categorical
labels emitted by the hierarchical transcriptomic classifier:

| column                          | example value     |
|---------------------------------|-------------------|
| `hier_class_assignment`         | `"Glutamatergic"` |
| `hier_neighborhood_assignment`  | `"IT_Types"`      |
| `hier_subclass_assignment`      | `"L2/3 IT"`       |
| `hier_cluster_assignment`       | `"L2/3 IT_1"`     |

Upstream sheets sometimes ship these labels with **spaces** (e.g.
`"L2/3 IT"`). Downstream code, including manuscript figure scripts, joins
on the *underscored* form (`"L2/3_IT"`) — that is the project convention.

Before this refactor the space → underscore replacement was being done in
several places (Seurat builder, manuscript setup, ad-hoc figure code),
each with slightly different defaults. The risk was obvious: stale or
unnormalised values silently surviving a join, then equality checks like
`hier_subclass_assignment == "L2/3_IT"` quietly returning `FALSE`.

### The helper

```r
proj5HT::normalize_hier_assignments(
  df,
  cols = c("hier_class_assignment",
           "hier_neighborhood_assignment",
           "hier_subclass_assignment",
           "hier_cluster_assignment")
)
```

For each column in `cols` that exists in `df`:

* `NA` and empty strings become `NA_character_`.
* Spaces are replaced with underscores (`fixed = TRUE`).

Idempotent and column-aware — safe to call multiple times.

### Where it is now wired

| Consumer                                   | How it gets normalized           |
|--------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| `hier5HT()`                                | calls the helper internally      |
| `add_hier_to_seurat()`                     | calls the helper internally; now also drops pre-existing hier cols before the merge so freshly-normalised values win |
| `dev/Seurat/Seurat_Objects.R` builder      | inline call right after the `cleanMD` join |
| `manu/_shared/setup.R::load_cohort_subclass()` | inline call after the `cleanMD` subset; `subclass_hier` is now an alias of the already-normalised `hier_subclass_assignment` |
| `data-raw/build_htr_scores.R`              | inline call right after `Seurat::FetchData()` so the persisted `htr_scores.csv/.rds` is always normalised |

If you ever add a new place that pulls hier labels out of `cleanMD` or a
Seurat object, call `normalize_hier_assignments()` once after the join
and move on.

---

## 2. What `build_htr_scores.R` actually produces

`data-raw/build_htr_scores.R` is the per-cell HTR (serotonin receptor)
expression table that backs manuscript figures 2, 3, 4, 6, and S2.

### Inputs

* `data-raw/Seurat/offPipeline-Macaque_PatchSeq.rds` — the curated
  macaque PatchSeq Seurat object. Its `counts` layer is already in
  **CPM** units, so it is *not* re-normalised here.
* `headBCI::sheets$cleanMD` — joined in via `add_hier_to_seurat()` so
  every cell carries the canonical hier classifier output.

### Output columns

```text
cell_name

# canonical hier classifier (headBCI) — now always normalised
hier_class_assignment
hier_neighborhood_assignment
hier_subclass_assignment
hier_cluster_assignment
hier_subclass_bootstrap_prob
hier_subclass_avg_corr

# legacy Seurat-internal calls (kept for QC comparison only)
subclass_Corr, subclass_Tree, cluster_Corr_label, predicted_subclass

# anatomical / QC
Cortical_area, Species, LIMS_depth, Depth_from_pia,
Genes.Detected, percent_reads_aligned_to_introns,
marker_sum_norm_label, quality_score_label, score.Corr_label

# per-receptor CPM (NA-tolerant)
HTR1A, HTR1B, HTR1D, HTR1E, HTR1F           # HTR1 family
HTR2A, HTR2B, HTR2C                         # HTR2 family
HTR4,  HTR5A, HTR6,  HTR7                   # "HTR0" family (other)

# provenance flags for known-iffy genes
htr1a_status, htr1b_status                  # "provisional_seurat_primary"

# derived per-cell scores
HTR1_sum, HTR2_sum, HTR0_sum
HTR_balance = HTR2_sum - HTR1_sum
```

### Interpretation

* **`HTR1_sum`** — total HTR1-family expression. The HTR1 family is
  predominantly **inhibitory** (Gi/o-coupled, hyperpolarising).
* **`HTR2_sum`** — total HTR2-family expression. The HTR2 family is
  predominantly **excitatory** (Gq-coupled, depolarising).
* **`HTR0_sum`** — bookkeeping sum of the remaining receptors
  (HTR4/5A/6/7), kept separate because they are mechanistically mixed.
* **`HTR_balance = HTR2_sum − HTR1_sum`** — single-number summary of
  whether a cell's receptor repertoire biases it toward **excitation by
  5-HT (> 0)** or **inhibition by 5-HT (< 0)**. This is the predictor we
  test against the empirically measured 5-HT response type (puff /
  drug-wash classification) for every figure that asks "does the
  transcriptomic receptor repertoire predict the physiological response?"

### Provisional values

`HTR1A` and `HTR1B` in the primary macaque Seurat object are
**known-inaccurate** (curation pending). Both rows are tagged via
`htr1a_status` / `htr1b_status = "provisional_seurat_primary"`. When the
accessory-file patch lands, those tags will flip (e.g.
`"patched_<source>_<date>"`) and the script can simply be re-run — all
the derived columns (`HTR1_sum`, `HTR_balance`) recompute from the new
values automatically.

### How the helper threads through

```r
df <- Seurat::FetchData(obj, vars = c(meta_cols, present))

## normalize hier assignment labels (single source of truth)
df <- proj5HT::normalize_hier_assignments(df)
```

So by the time `htr_scores.csv` hits disk, every `hier_*_assignment`
value matches what figure code expects (`"L2/3_IT"`, `"L5_IT"`,
`"L5_ET"`, …) — no matter whether the upstream `cleanMD` happened to use
spaces that day.

---

## 3. Quick sanity check

```r
library(proj5HT)

## load the built table
htr <- readRDS(here::here("data-raw/htr_scores.rds"))

## confirm normalisation took
unique(htr$hier_subclass_assignment)
#> "L2/3_IT" "L5_IT" "L5_ET" "L6_IT" ...   (no spaces)

## counts per subclass
table(htr$hier_subclass_assignment, useNA = "ifany")

## excitatory vs inhibitory bias by subclass
aggregate(HTR_balance ~ hier_subclass_assignment, htr, median, na.rm = TRUE)
```

---

## See also

* `?proj5HT::normalize_hier_assignments`
* `?proj5HT::hier5HT`
* `?proj5HT::add_hier_to_seurat`
* `data-raw/build_htr_scores.R`
* `manu/_shared/setup.R` — `load_cohort_subclass()`
* `dev/Seurat/Seurat_Objects.R` — `build_seurat_from_feather()`
